title: 第 79 節
en: As far as heaven reaches and as far as the sun reaches, so far does his world extend, and as long as the world of heaven and the sun does not decay, so long does his world not decay who thus knows the power of the eye.

6. By his ear the quarters and the moon were created. From all the quarters they come to him, and from all the quarters he hears, while the moon produces for him the bright and the dark halves for the sake of sacrificial work. Thus do the quarters and the moon serve their parent, the ear.
As far as the quarters reach and as far as the p 2 212 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
moon reaches, so far does his world extend, and as long as the world of the quarters and the moon does not decay, so long does his world not decay who thus knows the power of the ear.

7. By his mind the water and Varu;za were created. Water yields to him faith (being used for sacred acts), Varu^a keeps his offspring within the law. Thus do water and Varu/^a serve their parent, the mind.

As far as water reaches and as far as Varu^a reaches, so far does his world extend, and as long as the world of water and Varu^a does not decay, so long does his world not decay who thus knows the power of the mind.

Eighth Khaa-da K 1 . Was it water really ? Was it water ? Yes, all this was water indeed. This (water) was the root (cause), that (the world) was the shoot (effect). He (the person) is the father, they (earth, fire, &c.) are the sons. Whatever there is belonging to the son, belongs to the father ; whatever there is belonging to the father, belongs to the son. This was intended 2.

2. Mahidasa Aitareya, who knew this, said : ' I know myself (reaching) as far as the gods, and I know the gods (reaching) as far as me. For these 1 Having described how Praraa, the breath, and his companions or servants created the world, he now discusses the question of the material cause of the world out of which it was created. Water, which is said to be the material of the world, is explained by the commentator to mean here the five elements.

2 Cause and effect are not entirely separated, therefore water, as the elementary cause, and earth, fire, &c, as its effect, are one ; likewise the worshipper, as the father, and the earth, fire, &c. as his sons, as described above. Mula and tula, root and shoot, are evidently chosen for the sake of the rhyme, to signify cause and effect.
II ARAJVYAKA, I ADHYAYA, 8 KHANDA, 12. 213 gods receive their gifts from hence, and are supported from hence.'
3. This is the mountain1, viz. eye, ear, mind, speech, and breath. They call it the mountain of Brahman.
4. He who knows this, throws down the evil enemy who hates him ; the evil enemy who hates him is defeated.
5. He (the Pra/^a, identified with Brahman) is the life, the breath ; he is being (while the ^ivatman remains), and not-being (when the^ivatman departs).
6. The Devas (speech, &c.) worshipped him (prawa) as Bhuti or being, and thus they became great beings. And therefore even now a man who sleeps, breathes like bhurbhu/£.
7. The Asuras worshipped him as Abhiiti or notbeing, and thus they were defeated.
8. He who knows this, becomes great by himself, while the evil enemy who hates him, is defeated.
9. He (the breath) is death (when he departs), and immortality (while he abides).

• 10. And this has been said by a Rzshl (Rv. I, 11. ' Downwards and upwards he (the wind of the breath) goes, held by food ; ' — for this up-breathing, being held back by the down-breathing, does not move forward (and leave the body altogether).
1 2. ' The immortal dwells with the mortal ; ' — for through him (the breath) all this dwells together, the bodies being clearly mortal, but this being (the breath), being immortal.

1 Pra«a is called the g\nh, because it is swallowed or hidden by the other senses (girawat). Again a mere play of words, intended to show that Brahman under the form of Pra«a, or life, is to be meditated on.
214 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.

13. ' These two (body and breath) go for ever in different directions (the breath moving the senses of the body, the body supporting the senses of the breath : the former going upwards to another world, the body dying and remaining on earth). They increase the one (the body), but they do not increase the other,' i. e. they increase these bodies (by food), but this being (breath) is immortal.
14. He who knows this becomes immortal in that world (having become united with Hira^yagarbha), and is seen as immortal (in the sun) by all beings, yea, by all beings.
First Khanda.

1. He (the sun), who shines, honoured this world (the body of the worshipper, by entering into it), in the form of man 2 (the worshipper who meditates on breath). For he who shines (the sun) is (the same as) the breath. He honoured this (body of the worshipper) during a hundred years, therefore there are a hundred years in the life of a man. Because he honoured him during a hundred years, therefore there are (the poets of the first Mandala. of the Rigveda, called) the Satarkm, (having honour for a 1 In the first adhyaya various forms of meditating on Uktha, conceived as Pra»a (life), have been declared. In the second some other forms of meditation, all extremely fanciful, are added. They are of interest, however, as showing the existence of the hymns of the Rig-veda, divided and arranged as we now possess them, at the time when this Arawyaka was composed.
2 The identity of the sun and of breath as living in man has been established before. It is the same power in both, conceived either adhidaivatam (mythological) or adhyatmam (physiological).
II ARAiVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, I KKANDA, 6. 2 I 5 hundred years.) Therefore people call him who is really Prazza (breath), the 6atar/£in poets1.

2. He (breath) placed himself in the midst of all whatsoever exists. Because he placed himself in the midst of all whatsoever exists, therefore there are (the poets of the second to the ninth Maz^ala of the Rig-veda, called) the Madhyamas. Therefore people call him who is really Prazza (breath), the Madhyama poets.

3. He as up-breathing is the swallower (grz'tsa), as down-breathing he is delight (mada). Because as up-breathing he is swallower (grz'tsa) and as downbreathing delight (mada), therefore there is (the poet of the second Ma/^ala of the Rig-veda, called) Grztsamada. Therefore people call him who is really Pra/za (breath), G/z'tsamada.

4. Of him (breath) all this whatsoever was a friend. Because of him all (visvam) this whatsoever was a friend (mitram), therefore there is (the poet of the third Ma/za'ala of the Rig-veda, called) Vi^vamitra. Therefore people call him who is really Prazza (breath), Vijvamitra.
zh: 天空所達之處，太陽所照之地，他的世界便延伸至此；只要天空與太陽的世界不朽，那麼凡以此方式認知眼睛力量者的世界，亦不朽。
6. 四方與月亮是由耳朵創造的。從四方八面，它們來到耳朵，從四方八面，耳朵聽聞；而月亮為耳朵產生明與暗的半月，以供犧牲儀式之用。如此四方與月亮侍奉它們的親代——耳朵。
儘至四方所及，儘至 p 2 212《愛達羅耶奧義書》所及。
月亮所及之處，他的世界就延伸到那裡；只要四方與月亮的世界不衰敗，那麼懂得耳朵之力的人他的世界就不衰敗。
七、心靈創造了水與伐樓那（Varuṇa）。水向他讓步以信念（用於聖儀），伐樓那將其子嗣置於法則之中。如此，水與伐樓那侍奉其親代——心靈。
水所及之處，伐樓那（Varuṇa）所及之處，他的世界即延伸至彼；且只要水的世界與伐樓那的世界不衰敗，那麼如此認識心靈力量的人，其世界亦不衰敗。
第八卡亞達 K 1。那真的是水嗎？那是水嗎？是的，這一切確實都是水。這（水）是根（因），那（世界）是芽（果）。他（人）是父親，他們（地、火等）是兒子。凡屬於兒子的，都屬於父親；凡屬於父親的，都屬於兒子。這是有意設定的 2。
2. 瑪希達薩‧艾達瑞亞（Mahidasa Aitareya）知曉此事，遂言：「我認識我自己（達到）如同眾神一般，我認識眾神（達到）如同我自己一般。因為這些¹ 既已描述了普拉那（Prana）、氣息及其同伴或僕從如何創造了世界，他現在討論世界的物質因這一問題——世界是由此物質因創造而成的。據說構成世界的物質之水，評註者在此解釋為五大元素。
因果並非完全分離，因此水作為基本原因，而土、火等作為其結果，乃為一體；同樣地，如上所述，崇拜者作為父親，而土、火等作為其子嗣，亦為一體。Mula 和 tula（根與芽）顯然是為了押韻而被選中，用以表示因與果。
II ARAJVYAKA, I ADHYAYA, 8 KHANDA, 12. 213 諸提婆由此獲得其供奉，並由此得到庇護。
3. 這是那座山，即眼、耳、心、言語與呼吸。他們稱之為梵天之山。
4. 知此者制伏憎恨他的邪惡敵人；憎恨他的邪惡敵人被擊敗。
他（原人 Prajāpati，與梵天 Brahman 同一）即是生命，即是呼吸；他是有（當自我 Jīvātman 存在之時），也是無（當自我 Jīvātman 離去之時）。
6. 提婆神（言語等）崇拜他（prawa）作為不提（Bhuti）或存有，因而他們成為偉大的存有。是故即使在今日，睡眠中的人也呼吸如「bhurbhu/£」一般。
阿蘇羅（Asuras）將他視為阿比蒂（Abhiiti，非存在）而祭祀，因此他們遭致敗北。
8. 知曉此理者，自能偉大，而憎恨他的邪惡敵人，則遭挫敗。
9. 他（呼吸）是死亡（當他離去時），也是不朽（當他駐留時）。
• 10. 一位詩人（梨俱吠陀 I, 11）曾如此說道：「向下與向上而行，為食物所持；」——蓋此上升之氣息，為下降之氣息所制止，遂不得向前移動（而完全離開身體）。
1 2. 「不朽者與有朽者共住；」——蓋萬物因此（呼吸）而共存，身體雖明然有朽，但此存在（呼吸）乃不朽者。
Pra«a被稱為 g\nh，因為它被其他感官（girawat）所吞沒或隱蔽。再者，這不過是文字的遊戲，旨在說明梵天（Brahman）以 Pra«a（生命）的形式是應當被思考的對象。
214 愛達羅耶奧義書
13. 「這兩者（身體與呼吸）永遠朝向不同的方向前進（呼吸驅動身體的諸感官，身體支撐呼吸的諸感官：前者上升至另一世界，身體死亡而留在地上）。他們增加其一（身體），但不增加其他者，」亦即他們增加這些身體（藉由食物），但這個存有（呼吸）是不朽的。
14. 誰知此理，在彼世界即成不死者（已與金胎 *Hiraṇyagarbha* 合一），並為一切眾生所見為不死者（在太陽中），誠然，為一切眾生所見。
第一章

他（太陽）以人的形式照耀並尊崇了這個世界（禮拜者的身體，藉由進入其中），為的是那位照耀者（太陽），即呼吸的本身。他尊崇了這個（禮拜者的身體）長達一百年，因此人類的生命中便有了一百年。由於他尊崇了他一百年，因此便有了（梨俱吠陀第一曼陀羅的詩人，被稱為）《薩塔爾迦》（具有尊崇之意，為 1 在第一阿迪亞亞中，關於對烏克撒（Uktha）各種冥想的形式，被理解為普那那（Prana，生命），已經被闡述。在第二部分，其他若干冥想形式則被加入，全都極為幻想性。然而它們也引發興趣，因為它們顯示了梨俱吠陀的讚歌之存在，分門別類並按照我們當今所擁有的方式進行了編排，正是在這份阿蘭亞卡（Aranyaka）被撰寫時的樣貌。
太陽與人體內之呼吸的同一性已經確立無疑。兩者中存在的是同一種力量，只是分別以 adhidaivatam（神話層面）或 adhyatmam（生理層面）的方式來理解罷了。
II 阿蘭耶卡（Aranyaka）, 2 阿德亞亞（Adhyaya）, I 坎達（Kanda）, 6. 2 I五百年。）因此人們稱那位真正是普拉那（Prana，呼吸）的人為巴達林詩人（batarifin poets）1。
2. 他（氣息）將自己置於一切存在之物的中央。因為他將自己置於一切存在之物的中央，所以才有（梨俱吠陀第二至第九曼陀羅的詩人，被稱為）中間的詩人（Madhyamas）。因此人們稱真正的普拉札（氣息，Prazza）為中間的詩人。
3. 他作為上升之呼吸是吞嚥者（grz'tsa），作為下降之呼吸則是喜悅（mada）。因為作為上升之呼吸他是吞嚥者（grz'tsa），作為下降之呼吸則是喜悅（mada），所以才有（梨俱吠陀第二曼荼羅的詩人，被稱為）格茲薩瑪達（Grztsamada）。因此人們稱真正的普拉那（生氣 Pra/za，呼吸）者為格茲薩瑪達（G/z'tsamada）。
4. 他（呼吸）一切萬有都是友伴。因為他一切（visvam）萬有都是友伴（mitram），所以才有（梨俱吠陀第三曼陀羅的詩人，名叫）毗婆蜜多羅。因此人們稱呼那真實是普拉那（呼吸）的，為毗婆蜜多羅。
